zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：短期承压，震荡偏弱整理
zh.content:
目前SOL/USD价格徘徊在68.07附近，短期走势偏向震荡下行。虽然24小时微幅上涨0.50%，但价格仍低于20日SMA的69.36，显示多头动力不足，整体短期趋势承压。日内波动区间在67.5至68.13之间，市场仍在寻找方向。

技术指标显示，MACD为-0.998，处于零轴下方，表明空头动能占优。RSI暂不可用，但价格低于短期均线也暗示市场可能缺乏强烈买入信号。需关注指标交叉与零轴回归情况，以判断反弹或延续下行的可能性。

支撑位方面，可重点关注67.5和67.0区域，这两个水平若有效支撑，将形成短线买盘反应。阻力位主要在69.3附近，如突破将有助于短线反弹，但当前受制于均线压力，多头需谨慎进攻。

交易策略上，短线可采取区间操作策略，低位买入、高位减仓，控制仓位风险。中线投资者建议等待价格稳定突破20日SMA并伴随指标回暖后再考虑加仓，避免追高带来的潜在回撤。

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
SOL/USD is trading at 68.07, holding near the upper end of the intraday range between 67.50 and 68.13. Despite the modest 0.50% daily gain, the broader short-term structure remains under pressure as price continues to trade below the 20-period SMA at 69.36. This suggests sellers still maintain a degree of control, with recent rebounds appearing corrective rather than indicative of a confirmed trend reversal. Momentum will need to strengthen substantially for bulls to reclaim a more constructive outlook.

The moving average configuration highlights a mildly bearish bias. Trading below the SMA(20) indicates that short-term sentiment remains cautious, while the negative MACD reading of -0.998 points to lingering downside momentum. Although the signal line is unavailable, the negative MACD value alone suggests that bearish pressure has not fully dissipated. The absence of RSI data limits confirmation of overbought or oversold conditions, making price action around key technical levels especially important for directional clues.

Immediate support is located near the session low of 67.50, with additional downside interest likely emerging around the 67.00 area if selling pressure increases. A break below these levels could expose a deeper retracement toward the mid-66 region. On the upside, initial resistance is found at 68.13, followed by the more significant barrier at the 20-period SMA near 69.36. A sustained move above this moving average would improve the technical picture and potentially attract fresh buying interest.

From a trading perspective, conservative participants may prefer to wait for confirmation before establishing new positions. Bullish traders could look for a decisive breakout above 69.36 accompanied by stronger momentum signals, while bearish traders may view failed rallies beneath that level as opportunities to sell into resistance. Risk management remains essential, as the current environment reflects consolidation within a broader weak-to-neutral trend rather than a clearly established directional move.
